prose:
The Fukui reactivity analysis of acrolein was performed using DFT at the tier 'standard' level (B3LYP/def2-tzvp with density fitting) in the gas phase, with a charge of 0 and multiplicity of 1. The calculation was carried out using the PySCF backend.

The results indicate the most likely sites for electrophilic and nucleophilic attack:
*   The site most susceptible to **nucleophilic attack** (where f⁻ is maximal) is the oxygen atom (O1), with a Fukui index of **0.39389**.
*   The site most susceptible to **electrophilic attack** (where f⁺ is maximal) is the terminal carbon atom of the vinyl group (C4), with a Fukui index of **0.64232**.

The calculation converged successfully, and the engine reported that the results are trustworthy. No warnings were issued.

The full Fukui indices for each atom are as follows:

| Atom | Symbol | f⁻ (Nucleophilic) | f⁺ (Electrophilic) | f⁰ (Radical) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 1 | O | 0.39389 | 0.40136 | 0.39763 |
| 2 | C | 0.00599 | 0.02226 | 0.01412 |
| 3 | C | 0.09140 | 0.43405 | 0.26272 |
| 4 | C | 0.08737 | 0.64232 | 0.36485 |
| 5 | H | 0.08596 | -0.15726 | -0.03565 |
| 6 | H | 0.18568 | -0.20720 | -0.01076 |
| 7 | H | 0.08449 | -0.06212 | 0.01118 |
| 8 | H | 0.06523 | -0.07341 | -0.00409 |

Negative Fukui values can arise from the use of atom-centered basis functions and population analysis schemes (in this case, Mulliken) and are typically interpreted as regions of very low reactivity.
